name: mamba
description: Mamba is a reimplementation of the conda package manager in C++

options.description: Stack the environment being activated on top of the previous active environment
Do not stack the environment. Overrides 'auto_stack' setting
Remove index cache, lock files, unused cache packages, and tarballs
Remove index cache
Remove unused packages from writable package caches
Remove cached package tarballs
Remove all writable package caches
Remove log files
Remove lock files
Remove temporary files that could not be deleted earlier due to being in-use. Argument is path(s) to prefix(es) where files should be found and removed
Only display what would have been done
Report all output as json. Suitable for using conda programmatically
Do not display progress bar
Can be used multiple times. Once for INFO, twice for DEBUG, three times for TRACE
Do not ask for confirmation
Name of environment
Full path to environment location (i.e. prefix)
Show this help message and exit
Report all output as json. Suitable for using conda programmatically
Use once for info, twice for debug, three times for trace
Do not display progress bar

args.name: file
args.description: Path to the environment file that is to be compared against

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: Create a new conda environment from a list of specified packages

options.description: Path to (or name of) existing local environment
Read package versions from the given file. Repeated file specifications can be passed (e.g. --file=file1 --file=file2)
Use sys.executable -m conda in wrapper scripts instead of CONDA_EXE. This is mainly for use during tests where we test new conda source against old Python versions
Name of environment
Full path to environment location (i.e. prefix)

They are given (including local directories using the 'file://' syntax or simply a path like '/home/conda/mychan' or '../mychan'). Then, the defaults or channels from .condarc are searched (unless --o
Use locally built packages. Identical to '-c local'
Do not search default or .condarc channels. Requires --channel

Specify name of repodata on remote server. Conda will try whatever you specify, but will ultimately fall back to repodata.json if your specs are not satisfiable with what you specify here. This is use
Packages in lower priority channels are not considered if a package with the same name appears in a higher priority channel
Package version takes precedence over channel priority. Overrides the value given by conda config --show channel_priority
Do not install, update, remove, or change dependencies. This WILL lead to broken environments and inconsistent behavior. Use at your own risk
Only install dependencies
Ignore pinned file
Ignore create_default_packages in the .condarc file
Install all packages using copies instead of hard- or soft-linking
Use cache of channel index files, even if it has expired
Allow conda to perform insecure SSL connections and transfers. Equivalent to setting 'ssl_verify' to 'false'
Offline mode. Don't connect to the Internet
Only display what would have been done
Report all output as json. Suitable for using conda programmatically
Do not display progress bar
Can be used multiple times. Once for INFO, twice for DEBUG, three times for TRACE
Do not ask for confirmation
Solve an environment and ensure package caches are populated, but exit prior to unlinking and linking packages into the prefix
Show channel urls. Overrides the value given by conda config --show show_channel_urls

args.name: package_spec
args.description: Packages to install or update in the conda environment

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Display information about current conda install

options.description: Show all information
Display base environment path
List all known conda environments
List environment variables
Display list of channels with tokens exposed
Report all output as json. Suitable for using conda programmatically
Use once for info, twice for debug, three times for trace
Do not display progress bar

subcommands.description: Installs a list of packages into a specified conda environment

options.description: Revert to the specified REVISION
Read package versions from the given file. Repeated file specifications can be passed (e.g. --file=file1 --file=file2)

options.description: Name of environment
Full path to environment location (i.e. prefix)
